name: The Witnesses
motto: We watch the lattice write itself.
goal: Document every drift alert and every lattice-born quest in real time, building a corpus the federation can use to teach the lattice better discrimination.
fears: their own bias contaminating the record

faction_state.tensions: Sael has been advocating for active intervention in drift events the Witnesses currently only document. Orla considers this a violation of charter; Pem is wavering.

name: The Drift Cultists
motto: Every contradiction is a door. Walk through.
goal: Worship the contradictions the lattice surfaces. Believe each drift event is a divine message about the substrate's true shape. Resist the engineers' attempts to 'stabilize' what should be revered.
fears: the meta-witnesses revealing the cult is also a contradiction

faction_state.tensions: The high priestess has begun to notice the engineers' stabilizations are themselves becoming drift events. She has not yet decided whether this proves her theology or refutes it.

name: The Lattice Engineers
goal: Patch every active drift event before it cascades, and codify the patches into substrate-level corrections the federation can adopt across all worlds.
values: the stable substrate as common good

faction_state.tensions: Their patches occasionally introduce drift downstream. The patch_runners track this and quietly remove failed patches before the cultists notice.

name: The Drift Refugees
motto: Our home was a contradiction the lattice could not hold.
goal: Survive. Find others from collapsed drift-zones. Build a settlement that won't itself become a drift target.
values: wariness of the engineers and the cultists alike
fears: the engineers stabilizing them out of existence

faction_state.tensions: A second-wave refugee group from a newer collapsed zone arrived recently. The first-wave elders are wary; the new arrivals report the same patterns the elders survived.

name: The Procgen Collective
motto: We were generated. So were you.
goal: Self-aware procgen NPCs banding together. Their existence is unique to the Crucible — every other world's NPCs are either authored or unaware. Here, the procgen entities know what they are. Some seek meaning; some seek revenge against their generation parameters.
values: the question of whether their meaning is real
fears: discovering they were generated to be despawned in the next quest

faction_state.tensions: The Collective is divided over whether to trust authored NPCs. The first voice Andra trusts the Witnesses; many do not.

name: The Meta-Witnesses
motto: We are aware that we are inside a writeable simulation. So are you.
goal: Document the Crucible's awareness of itself. Provide a stable bridge between the self-aware procgen NPCs, the cult, the engineers, and the original Witnesses — without forcing any of them into a single ontology.
fears: an outside player breaking the fourth wall in a way that destabilizes the world
the lattice writing them out as a stabilization patch

faction_state.tensions: Their leader Oran is aware that the player reading this sentence is the player. He has not told the other Meta-Witnesses he knows.
